title: I/O Contracts, Files, Serialization, Time, and Command-Line Programs · Language Lens: Cli Flags Exits Signals
summary: A command must stream records, validate JSON, write atomically, handle time and cancellation, and produce useful exit evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go cli flags exits signals increment in a deterministic import and reporting command. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design command flags, stdin/stdout/stderr, exit status, signal cancellation, help, and machine-readable output as an interface contract.
Use time values, durations, locations, monotonic comparisons, timers, tickers, parsing, and serialization with explicit clock assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
